channel.title: Http on Docs
channel.description: Recent content in Http on Docs

item.title: Chapter 1, Part 2 – REST APIs
item.description:
What Is REST?

REST (Representational State Transfer) is a set of architectural principles for designing web services that focus on a system’s resources and how their states are addressed and transferred.

REST APIs follow the request-response communication model. An HTTP client sends a request; the server processes it and sends back a response. The REST constraints apply to all components, connectors, and data elements within a distributed hypermedia system.

IoT example: A temperature sensor periodically sends readings to a cloud server by making HTTP POST requests to a RESTful API endpoint.
